PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Thibeault in Propria Persona.
David Baer of Hartog, Baer & Hand.
Emilie Calhoun for Patricia Dark and Mary Connolly.

The Court noted that the substitution of attorney form on its face indicates that it can not be used in estate cases leaving the personal representative to act on their own and that there should have been a motion to withdraw as counsel. Ms. Calhoun stated that not only is the Estate a party in a creditor's claim that went to a civil action, but it is also a cross-defendant in a partition action that was initiated by Mr. Thibeault. Ms. Calhoun also stated that her Senior Associate is now with Mr. Baer's firm and that Mr. Baer can not represent Mr. Thibeault. Mr. Baer stated that ever since Mr. Parnell came to his firm, he could no longer perform any services for Mr. Thibeault. Ms. Calhoun stated that there is another Attorney that consistently has been assisting Mr. Thibeault. Mr. Thibeault stated that there are no assets in the estate, that he represented the estate for the two civil matters, asked to have until the end of March and stated that he will have another Attorney by that point. The Petition for Approval of Status of Administration is under submission. The Court will allow continued administration based on the representation that Mr. Thibeault will obtain new Counsel which is to be done by the time the Court allows (probably three months).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, To Paul Thibeault, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-22-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie M. Calhoun, Counsel for Petitioner, Patricia Dark; David Baer, Counsel for Personal Representative and Respondent, Paul L. Thiebeault.

The Court notes the tentative ruling [denying the motion] to which Ms. Calhoun responds. Assuming the tentative ruling is affirmed, she would request clarification as to (1) whether the the ruling is with or without prejudice to this or other actions and (2) whether the Court will strike Exhibit 9 from the respondent's opposition papers as violative of the mediation confidentiality agreement.

Aside from these procedural concerns, she notes that there are pending objections to Petitioner's third-party subpoenas which will be prolonged by the Court's tentative ruling. Additionally, Ms. Calhoun argues that there currently exists a "contested" matter as evidenced by the opposition to the motion and that a creditor's claim is a "special proceeding" within the meaning of the Code of Civil Procedure.

Mr. Baer responds, contending that a creditor's claim is not a special proceeding within the meaning of the code, citing various authority. Believing the motion was completely without merit, Mr. Baer renews his request for sanctions in the amount of $2,187.50, representing his time and cost in opposing the motion.

After further reply, the Court denies the motion as there is no "contested proceeding" before it which would permit formal discovery. That said, the Court re-emphasizes its desire to encourage the parties to co-operate in the extra-judicial exchange of information to expedite a resolution of the underlying matter.

Given Mr. Baer's stipulation, the Court strikes Exhibit 9 from the Declaration of David Baer filed in opposition to the pending motion and notes that it did not conside the exhibit in deciding this motion.

The Court emphasizes that it is deciding this motion to compel with respect to the creditor's claim in its current posture only and no other matter or other posture.

The Court reaffirms its tentative ruling with respect to sanctions and denies the same.

Mr. Baer to prepare the order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Jaime; Reporter: Michelle McLaughlin, CSR# 9829, American Reporting Services, 800-624-8688.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Mediation.
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for and with Patricia Dark (Petitioner/Respondent).
David Baer for and with Paul Thibeault (Objector).
Edward Koplowitz for Scott Hildula (Guardian Ad Litem for Morgan Hildula) and Joseph Hildula.

Ms. Calhoun raised the issue of no Trustee being appointed to the Children's Trust, nine document demands not having been produced, raised the issue of conflict of interest with Mr. Thibeault serving as Administrator, stated that the issues stated above are prerequisites to resuming mediation and raised the possibility of a neutral being appointed. Mr. Baer stated that he is prepared to have the matter submitted, that he would like to get the petitions resolved, that he is not ruling out mediation and argued against waiting until someone be appointed in the Trust matter. Mr. Koplowitz asked that the matter scheduled for January 24th be continued out further, pointed out that there are five pending petitions regarding one building and stated that he would like to get back into mediation as soon as possible. The petitions on calendar for January 24, 2017 in the Ann Hildula Trust PTR-16-300269 are continued to February 7, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Calhoun stated that she will be withdrawing two and amending one. Status re mediation is also continued to February 7, 2017. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Probate of Will, etc. are taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Dawn Stark, CSR #7847, 415-362-4346.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Continued To Oct-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun, for and with petitioner Patricia Dark; William O'Connell and Tom Shaver for Paul Thibeault. Ed Koplowitz was initially present for Scott Hildula, but left before the hearing commenced.

Ms. Calhoun stated three representations on behalf of Guardian ad litem Scott Hildula at the request of Ed Koplowitz: 1) the guardian ad litem Scott Hildula objects to the appointment of Paul Thibeault as administrator; 2) Mr. Hildula does not object to the appointment of a neutral third party as administrator; and 3) Mr. Koplowitz suggests that the matter go to mediation. Ms. Calhoun said that her client agreeswith the mediation suggestion. She also contended that Paul Thibeault's memorandum of points and authorities are not supported by a verified declaration and accordingly should not be considered. She further contended that Paul Thibeault's interests are in conflict with the other beneficiaries and the estate, and therefore his petition for appointment should be denied. Mr. Shaver stated that the only issue is who should be appointed as administrator of the estate. He said that Mr. Thibeault has priority both under the express terms of the will and by statute. The XCourt asked if Mr. Thibeault is open to mediation of the dispute. Mr. Shaver stated that his client is open to mediation, as well as to appointment of a neutral administrator. As all parties agreed to participate in mediation, the Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 12, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 19, 2016. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on October 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The hearing on the petitions was continued to the same date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. Reporter: Juvilynn Benasfre, CSR #13817.
